name: Veggie Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
1. Place tortillas on a flat surface for filling.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
3. Cook onions and garlic until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Add canned tomatoes, tomato sauce, Tabasco sauce, chili powder, maple syrup, and a pinch of salt to the pan. Stir and cook until well mixed and heated through.
5. In another skillet, heat remaining oil over medium heat.
6. Cook mashed pinto beans, garlic, and half a cup of chopped onions in the oil until heated through.
7. Add black olives and a pinch of chili powder. If the bean mixture is too thick, add some of the sauce mixture from step 4.
8. Assemble enchiladas by spreading about 2-3 tablespoons of bean mixture on one edge of each tortilla, not in the center (rolling up makes it easier).
9. Roll up the tortillas tightly and place them seam-side down in a shallow baking dish.
10. Cover with remaining sauce and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until bubbly. Serve hot. Makes 6-8 servings. Optional: Dot enchiladas with cheesy nutritional yeast topping.
